title: practical english usage - Part 102
content:
They had little difficulty in stealing the painting.

Nouns     and   Noun    Phrases;    Agreement grammar •  121   piece- and group-words: a blade of grass; a bunch offlowers For an amount, a lot, a large number, etc, ►     172.   For sort, type, kind, etc, ►    592.

For a bit as a modifier before adjectives and adverbs, ►    406.

spanners (AmE  wrenches) a set of napkins/dishes/cutlery (AmE flatware/silverware)/ contain a fixed number of things.

Set is used before many uncountable and plural nouns referring to groups which a herd of cattle/goats a pack of cards (AmE a deck of cards) a bunch offlowers  a crowd of people  a flock of sheep/birds collections.

Special words are used before certain plural nouns to talk about groups or 4 plural     nouns:    collections a pair of seissors/pliers/tweezers a pair of trousers/jeans/shorts/pyjamas/leggings/tights (BrE)/pantyhose (AmE) a pair of shoes/boots/socks/earrings a pair of glasses/binoculars that refer to some two-part objects (►    117.7).

Pair is used for many things that normally go in twos, and with plural nouns 3 pair He came downstairs without a stitch of clothing on.

We haven't got a scrap/bite (of food) to eat.

There hasn't been a breath of air all day.

There's not a grain of truth in what he says.

'no ... at all'.

Some words for small pieces can be used in a negative structure meaning 2  not    a  ...  of  ...

a length of material                a strip of cloth/tape/land an item of information/news/elothing/ furniture  a stick of dynamite/chalk/m a grain of sand/salt/rice/corn/dust/truth      a sheet of paper/me tal/plasu a drop of water/oil/vinegar            a speck of dust a blade of grass                  a slice of bread/cake/meat a bar of chocolate/soap              a lump of sugar/coal common examples: Other words are less general, and are used before particular nouns. Some a piece/bit of news/information ɑ  piece/bit of cake/bread  some pieces/bits of paper/wood this kind are piece and bit. Bit (informal) suggests a small quantity.

unit, together with of, before an uncountable noun. The  most general words of To  talk about a limited quantity of something we  can use a word for a piece or 1  uncountable         nouns:    pieces a  bunch       of   flowers       ________________ 121  piece-    and   group-words:        a  blade      of   grass; grammar •  123   possessives: noun + 's (forms) an old people's home irregular plural + 's  the children's room, men's clothes, women's rights, plural noun + '           my parents' house singular noun + ’ s       my father's car 1  spelling they express other ideas as well as possession.

Forms like John's, parents', children's are generally called ‘ possessive’ , although 123 possessives:       noun    + 's (forms)   ________ For relative clauses after nouns (e.g. the people who live next door), ►    Section 21.

For prepositions before clauses, ►     210.

For the prepositions that are used after some common nouns, ►    213.

For should in clauses after nouns, ►    264.6.   For subjunctives in clauses after nouns, ►    232.

For more information about -ing forms after nouns, ►     103.   For infinitives after nouns, ►     102.

a particular noun. It is best to check  in a good dictionary.

Unfortunately, there is no easy way to decide which  structures are possible after For details of noun + noun structures, ►     123-126.

my father's company  garden furniture Nouns (possessive or not) can also act as modifiers of following nouns.

I do not intend to resign.

I have no intention of resigning.

Note that a related noun and verb may have different kinds of complement.

(          ffeedonvofttlwosing) freedom of choice - freedom to choose the idea that I might get married - the idea of marriage Not all nouns can be followed by all kinds of complement.

The main reason why I don't believe her is this:...

You've no reason to get angry.

He didn't give any reason for the changes.

Many nouns can be followed by more than one kind of complement.

There's still the question of whether we're going to pay her.
